iti100:1.2: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti100:1.3: "I am a brahman, responsive to requests, open-handed, bearing my last body, an unsurpassed doctor and surgeon. You are my children, my sons, born from my mouth, born of the Dhamma, created by the Dhamma, heirs to the Dhamma, not heirs in material things.
iti100:1.4: "There are these two kinds of gifts: a gift of material things and a gift of the Dhamma. Of the two, this is supreme: a gift of the Dhamma.
iti100:1.5: "There are these two kinds of sharing: sharing of material things and sharing of the Dhamma. Of the two, this is supreme: sharing of the Dhamma.
iti100:1.6: "There are these two kinds of assistance: assistance with material things and assistance with the Dhamma. Of the two, this is supreme: help with the Dhamma.
iti100:1.7: "There are these two kinds of mass-donations: a mass-donation of material things and a mass-donation of the Dhamma. Of the two, this is supreme: a mass-donation of the Dhamma."
iti100:1.8: He who, unstinting, made the mass-donation of Dhamma, the Tathagata, sympathetic to all beings: to one of that sort — the best of beings, human and divine — living beings pay homage — to one gone to the beyond of becoming.
iti100:1.9: This, too, was the meaning of what was said by the Blessed One, so I have heard.
